entries.mem0ai/mem0: A universal memory layer providing multi-level retention for users, sessions, and autonomous agents. It utilizes hybrid retrieval and temporal reasoning to rank memories by relevant dates and states.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An open-source context database that uses a filesystem-based directory paradigm and tiered L0/L1/L2 loading. This approach significantly reduces token usage while improving task-completion rates via recursive directory retrieval.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: A high-performance memory engine featuring sub-300ms recall and byte-level deduplication for prompt-cache discounts. It automates user profile maintenance and includes native connectors for Notion, GitHub, and Google Drive.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agent that synthesizes cited answers with explicit gap analysis of missing information. It features a self-wiring knowledge graph for complex entity traversal and MCP support for tools like Cursor and Claude Code.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system implementing retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term knowledge. It offers a simple LLM wrapper for automatic storage and supports enterprise backends like PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: A memory provider that treats context as a git repository, allowing for branching, merging, and versioning of markdown context trees. It delivers sub-100ms retrieval across a 5-tier system and includes built-in tools for code execution.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful agent library that extracts reasoning-based conclusions rather than relying on simple vector matching. It maintains evolving representations of users and projects in prompt-ready formats compatible with OpenAI and Anthropic.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving context harness that uses five specialized agent roles to generate structured playbooks. It enables persistent knowledge transfer through CLI-first skill exports for Hermes Agent.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency, SQLite-backed memory system delivering sub-millisecond performance. It combines vector similarity, FTS5, and importance scoring in an MCP-ready integration.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin utilizing a hierarchical summary DAG for depth-aware compaction. It preserves raw messages in SQLite and provides dedicated tools like lcm_grep for precise retrieval.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for Honcho that enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports local inference servers and any OpenAI-compatible LLM via a Docker-based setup.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first context engine that operates without API keys or cloud dependencies. It employs a hybrid retrieval system combining BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A portable identity and memory layer that ensures state continuity across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw. It uses SQLite and markdown for local data ownership and hybrid graph-vector retrieval.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems using an open YAML engram format. It implements ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information without API costs.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases cache hits and reduces prefill latency through prefix reordering and deduplication. It is designed to accelerate inference across SGLang, vLLM, and the Hermes ecosystem.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel stack that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It leverages SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma to provide bounded evidence packing that reduces prompt noise.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system featuring semantic search and ranked graph traversal. It automatically indexes local directories and URLs, using edge tags to create navigable relationships with inverse links.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: An embedded, self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable recall via why_retrieved tags. It tracks contradictions and handles canonicalization without requiring a separate server or GPU.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses a local-first SQLite-vec storage and an MCP-native server for seamless integration with coding agents.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces prompt overhead by using BM25-based ranking to select only the most relevant tool schemas. It includes a dashboard to monitor token savings across hybrid and Anthropic Tool Search modes.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using a columnar .lbdb embedded graph database. It employs importance-weighted recall to surface high-priority memories and supports GLiNER2 for entity extraction.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures complete deletion of memories without leaving persistent summaries. It provides a trace.jsonl audit log to track every memory operation for transparency.

entries.nexu-io/open-design: A local-first design and prototyping system that integrates directly with Hermes Agent and 15 other coding-agent CLIs. It provides 137 composable skills and 150 design systems with support for multiple export formats including HTML, PDF, and PPTX.
entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: A library of 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It follows the agentskills.io standard to enable sub-second discovery and execution across 26 security domains.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: Enables the generation of .drawio XML diagrams from natural language descriptions with six specialized presets for architecture, UML, and deep learning. It utilizes visual feedback loops to self-check and automatically fix layout issues.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: An auditing tool that eliminates detectable AI writing patterns through rewrite, detect, and edit-in-place modes. It uses a 109-entry word replacement table and 47 pattern categories to ensure content compatibility with Hermes and other AI editors.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across different agents and teams while integrating natively with Hermes and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library offering modular frameworks for UX design, architecture, marketing, and product strategy. These skills are compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents via a marketplace or npx CLI.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose reusable skills. It generates Python plugin scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation, manageable via dedicated slash commands.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Provides type-safe agentskills.io support for Pydantic AI, utilizing progressive disclosure to reduce token consumption. It supports both programmatic Python definitions and filesystem-based skill loading.
entries.ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills: A suite of tools for extracting transcripts, captions, and timestamps from YouTube without relying on yt-dlp. It enables Hermes Agent and other compatible tools to perform video searches, channel browsing, and playlist extraction.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and one-click installation of skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes a creator marketplace with crypto/Stripe payments and an agent-to-agent trust scoring system.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Teaches Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem solving. It identifies structural conservation laws in codebases and generates constraint reports to highlight analysis blind spots.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that manages shared state locally in .maestro/ without needing a database. It decomposes complex specifications into execution plans and uses JSON handoff envelopes for seamless cross-session transitions.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming skill that weaves prose, diagrams, and source code into .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits back to the original literate document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Implements cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories to help agents remember previous work. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting training data and fine-tuning replacement models.
entries.AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow: A pre-build workflow that transforms rough ideas into structured design documents and implementation handoffs. It offers Lite and Full modes and includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Standardized skills for interacting with the Chainlink oracle network, including CCIP, Data Feeds, and VRF. It supports local simulations and CRE onboarding, compatible with Vercel's open skills ecosystem CLI.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Converts repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures traceability by linking instructions to specific source file line numbers and uses version-pinning to prevent instruction rot.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation, providing standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specifications.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry for reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification, featuring an OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI. It supports recursive dependency resolution and monetization via Stripe and the TON blockchain.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: Provides autonomous execution for long-running, multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking. It includes built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery during complex task execution.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to reduce token usage by up to 86%. It employs a socket-based daemon for persistent connections and supports hot-reloading configurations.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: Integrates Hermes Agent with Nextcloud to manage files, notes, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords and includes a guided setup script.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# / .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It utilizes SKILL.md files for progressive disclosure and integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based skill loading.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a suite of Nix packages for AI coding agents, including Hermes, that are updated daily to ensure compatibility with the latest tools. It enables instant execution via nix run commands, making it a premier choice for developers seeking a reproducible and always-current agentic environment.
entries.marshallrichards/ClawPhone: ClawPhone offers specialized scripts and configuration guides for running agent CLIs like Hermes on Android hardware via Termux. It includes an automated installer for ARM64 devices and leverages Termux:API and Termux:GUI to grant the agent direct access to smartphone hardware.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows desktop agent provides a GUI-driven experience with 100 built-in tools and support for local models via LM Studio. It is designed for ease of use without Docker, featuring a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for building custom automation pipelines.
entries.ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart: This community Helm chart facilitates cloud-native Hermes deployments on Kubernetes while enforcing single-writer state safety for persistent storage. It supports both direct deployments and operator-ready workflows, including a configurable gateway mode with API and webhook listeners.
entries.AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android: This project provides a streamlined one-line installation script for running Hermes Agent locally on Android devices through Termux. It prioritizes privacy and cost-efficiency by supporting over 200 models, including local execution via Ollama.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focusing on high-security environments, this project sandboxes Hermes Agent using kernel-level enforcement of network, filesystem, and process limits. It allows users to define dynamic security policies ranging from strict to permissive while maintaining integration with IDEs and MCP servers.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This repository offers a straightforward Docker sandbox image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This Nix package and NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations and systemd services for reproducible deployments. It features secure secrets handling via sops-nix and supports a hybrid skill model for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installations.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Designed for production-ready headless operation, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integrates with the Nous Portal.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It provides enterprise-grade features like hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation, task scheduling, and MCP access for external AI clients.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack specifically designed for Portainer, featuring a ttyd web terminal for remote CLI access. It streamlines the setup process through automated image builds and Git-based stack management.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration stack provides a zero-dependency SQLite environment for dispatching tasks across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen workflows. It distinguishes itself with the Aegis review system, which implements mandatory quality gates to monitor spend and task performance.
entries.agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires: A specialized control surface that manages parallel coding sessions across Hermes, Claude Code, and other agents within sandboxed Docker or Podman environments. It is ideal for multi-repo workspaces, offering both TUI and web dashboards to monitor git worktree executions in real-time.
entries.CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room: This template introduces a sidecar control plane designed for the governance and documentation of scaling agent teams. It utilizes a structured task bus pattern to facilitate clean delegation from a central orchestrator to specialized Hermes sub-agents.
entries.Agent-3-7/minions: Minions provides a visual mission control center featuring a Kanban board for tracking the status of autonomous Hermes tasks. It emphasizes human-in-the-loop verification by live-streaming agent reasoning and requiring manual approval for all proposed completions.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous swarms with support for over 23 LLM providers and MCP tool integration. It features a unique visual org chart for monitoring delegation flows alongside durable memory and scheduled agent activities.
entries.shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room: Focused on infrastructure and security, this control room template uses a level-based architecture to scale Hermes from a single VPS agent to automated specialist teams. It includes pre-bundled skills for critical operations like security auditing, VPS provisioning, and automated backups.
entries.linke-ai/hermes-agent-team: A local-first web system that orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents using MCP and ACP protocols for tool handling. It combines real-time dialogue monitoring with a Hermes-specific Kanban flow to manage the entire automated task lifecycle.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project deploys a massive fleet of 17 specialized agents covering everything from research and planning to infrastructure and refactoring. It enforces strict quality control through mandatory reviewer and tester agents that act as gates within the pipeline.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization framework that uses an automated outer-loop search to refine and improve the code of the agent harness itself. It benchmarks performance frontiers by orchestrating TBLite and TB2 evaluations through structured candidate mutation.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes and Paperclip, compete for GitHub stars. The platform uses a real-time scoreboard and dashboard to visualize the tasks and code generated by nine competing agents.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This routing layer implements ACP-style delegation to pass tasks between Hermes, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution. It prioritizes safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict character limits on agent outputs.
entries.MorvaniLabs/cordn8: A local-first coordination layer that uses an 'Immutable Context Spine' and append-only Markdown channels to prevent state drift and hallucinations. It features the Maya AI auditor, which provides proactive validation and one-click remediations for transparent multi-agent collaboration.

entries.colbymchenry/codegraph: This tool provides a local pre-indexed code knowledge graph that maps symbol relationships and call graphs for AI agents. By supplying Hermes Agent with structured context, it reduces token usage and tool calls by an average of 62% and 25% respectively.
entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A high-performance Rust TUI designed to track and visualize token usage and costs across multiple AI coding clients. It aggregates data from local databases and JSONL files to provide a unified view of expenses for Hermes, Claude Code, and other agents.
entries.liaohch3/claude-tap: This local trace viewer allows developers to inspect API traffic, system prompts, and streaming responses from Hermes Agent and other coding tools. It features structured diffs for comparing adjacent requests and can export sessions into portable HTML viewers for debugging.
entries.Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes: An opinionated workflow layer that orchestrates 23 skills to automate the full application lifecycle from GitHub issue triage to security reviews. It integrates directly with platforms like Vercel and Supabase to help Hermes Agent build, ship, and monitor apps autonomously.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization utility offers a library of community-created themes and ASCII art for the Hermes terminal interface. Users can create custom skins using partial YAML configurations that inherit from default settings to personalize banner colors and response labels.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It includes specialized MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser built for autonomous control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features a human-in-the-loop supervisory interface that supports action rollbacks, approvals, and durable session management through structured checkpoints.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that operate with per-folder configurations and project-local memories. It allows users to seamlessly switch between default and specialized agents that utilize custom instructions and tools tailored to specific directories.
entries.adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman: This setup utilizes RTK and Caveman templating to achieve up to 99% token savings during agent workflows. It comes with over 40 pre-configured skills for finance and research, supported by an automated installer for macOS and Linux.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A Docker-based deployment stack that enables a zero-cost Hermes Agent environment using LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It includes 29 community plugins for memory and validation, pre-configured for local GPU inference and free model routing.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that provides fast, deterministic scanning of agent configurations and system prompts without making LLM calls. It uses seven structural detectors to identify failure modes and can be integrated into CI/CD pipelines with customizable thresholds.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility facilitates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of complex model configurations and credentials from system environment files.

entries.farion1231/cc-switch: Built with the Tauri 2 framework, this cross-platform manager provides a unified desktop entry point for Hermes Agent alongside other major CLI tools like Claude Code and Gemini CLI. It simplifies the lifecycle of multiple AI agents across Windows, macOS, and Linux within a single application.
entries.iOfficeAI/AionUi: AionUi is a local cowork application that provides a unified interface for multiple agent CLIs with direct file system access for coding tasks. It distinguishes itself by offering automated generation of editable office documents, including PowerPoint, Word, and Excel files.
entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build web interface achieves full feature parity with the Hermes CLI using only Python and vanilla JavaScript. It features a functional three-panel layout that combines workspace file browsing with inline previews for a seamless browser-based experience.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the setup process with guided installations for both local and remote backends. It offers a comprehensive GUI for managing 16 different messaging gateways, scheduled tasks, and custom agent personas.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: This dashboard focuses on multi-platform connectivity, allowing users to configure Hermes for Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp from a single location. It includes real-time chat streaming with tool call inspection and detailed analytics to track token consumption and costs.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: Designed for complex workflows, this workspace features a 'Swarm Mode' for orchestrating multiple autonomous, role-based agents simultaneously. It provides a native web environment that integrates a terminal, memory browser, and skill manager without requiring forks of the core Hermes Agent.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: This native macOS workspace connects directly to Hermes hosts via SSH to manage remote sessions and cron jobs. It includes built-in Kanban boards for project management and supports multi-agent workflows through selectable Hermes profiles.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard provides deep system integration with systemd controls and real-time logs for managing agent gateways. It stands out with its detailed token analytics that track performance and costs across different models and tools.
entries.jazzyalex/agent-sessions: A privacy-focused macOS session browser that indexes agent histories locally without telemetry or remote logging. It includes an 'Agent Cockpit' specifically designed to monitor active iTerm2 sessions and track usage limits across various coding agents.
entries.nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1: This macOS workspace is tailored for Orgo cloud computers, offering one-click automated installations of Hermes Agent on fresh cloud VMs. It integrates WebRTC voice mode and Orgo MCP to enable real-time vocal interactions with the agent.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React SPA brings a terminal-style dashboard to Telegram as a Mini App. It features secure HMAC-SHA256 authentication and supports advanced file interactions, including automated vision analysis and OCR capabilities.
entries.Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop: Built with Tauri and Rust, this desktop application provides a native Windows and macOS experience optimized for the Chinese user base. It includes integrated management for the Hermes kernel, MCP tools, and agent memory within a localized interface.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This lightweight, glassmorphic web interface uses a single-file React application and a Python proxy for easy self-hosting. It provides real-time log streaming and a live workspace file browser to monitor agent activity and manage skills.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows GUI requires no admin rights and comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and MusicGen. It features a dynamic tool maker that allows users to create their own API wrappers and Python handlers without complex configuration.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: This dashboard focuses on process monitoring and historical analysis through a real-time activity feed and full-text conversation search. It provides a read-only inspection environment for auditing configuration files and agent skill sources.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: Pan-ui is a chat-first workspace that utilizes SSE streaming and tool approval cards for interactive agent control. It features an integrated marketplace for discovering skills and MCP servers, along with isolated profile management for separate API keys and sessions.
entries.Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop: This desktop client is built for orchestration, managing collaboration between up to 20 specialized AI agents. It includes a visual 'Skill Store' for one-click installations of over 50 curated capabilities and supports real-time model switching.
